title: MetaVir- Analysis of viromes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"257vv","text":"METAVIR is a web server designed to annotate viral metagenomic sequences (raw reads or assembled contigs). A set of published viromes, identified as \"public projects\", is already available, and your own data sets can be processed in a private environment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: First select a project. a
Then select a virome. a
First tab links to the virome overview page. a
The next tab links to the taxonomic composition page. The different taxonomic compositions computed are all displayed through this page. a
Krona makes it possible to navigate through the piechart diagram. a
All different ratios are displayed on the right. a
Different types of taxonomic composition are automatically computed. More details on: http://metavir-meb.univ-bpclermont.fr/index.php?page=Taxo
A comparison of viromes taxonomic composition is available a
First pick a type of composition. a
Then choose a type of visualization. a
Then select all the viromes you want to compare. a
Affiliation rates are reminded here. a
Several compositions can be displayed on the same interactive Krona chart. a
The same comparison can be displayed through a heatmap a
This is an interactive heatmap. a
Fold and unfold taxonomic groups with a mouse click. a
Font size can be modified to fit your screen. a
Drag and drop columns to modify their order. a
The heatmap can be downloaded as a csv file. a
This button links to the recruitment plot. a
Available genomes are classified by taxonomic group. a
Total number of reads available is indicated here and the number of reads for each group/genome is displayed here. a
On this first type of recruitment plot each read is displayed as a dot (scatter plot). a
A genome map is displayed at the bottom with genes name when possible. a
This is a list of potential list of viromes to add to the recruitment plot, the number of reads is between brackets. a
Each virome is identified by a specific color. a
Plots of type histogram display the number of hits for 500-bp wide regions along the genome. a
Plots can be downloaded in different file formats. a
A table of number of hits for each gene is also available. a
Finally, sequences of reads associated with the recuitment plot can be downloaded in fasta format. a
Generating the file package can take up to a few minutes.
This tab links to the phlyogeny page. a
Different marker genes are available. a
For each marker and each virome, a tree gallery is displayed. Leaves are colored according to the taxonomy, except for metagenomic sequences highlighted in red and bold. a
Trees can be displayed in circular or linear form. a
Information is displayed on each leaf when clicked. a
Internal nodes can be gathered and unfolded with a mouse click. a
Picture tree can be downloaded as a svg file. a
The tree gallery is browsed through these two buttons. a
A package containing all sequences used in the trees as well as the alignments and trees as text and picture is available to download. a
A static tree gallery is also available. a
Trees can be displayed on fullscreen. a
The fullscreen view is especially useful when dealing with large trees.
Trees can be computed from multiple viromes if the trees with the same marker were computed for each dataset. a
Leaves are then colored according to the virome, reference sequences being left gray. a
Taxonomy of reference sequences is still indicated next to the leaf name. a
This tab links to rarefaction curves. a
Three clustering thresholds are available that can be used with whole virome or normalized sub-samples (50,000 sequences, 100bp) a
Curve colors are associate with the virome biome. a
A range of dots on each curve makes it possible to display the virome name when hovered. a
Curves can be colored by virome. a
The matrix used to plot the curve is available to download via this link. a
This button links to virome comparison. a
Comparisons can be based on k-mer frequencies bias or tBLASTx reads similarities. a
An MDS can be computed to display the different viromes. a
Matrix and plot can be downloaded. a
Same comparison can be displayed through a clustering tree. a
New tools were developed for contigs.
They are automatically loaded when a project of type "contigs" is selected. a
Three types of taxonomic composition are computed for contigs: Predicted gene best Blast hit Contig best BLAST hit Contig lowest common ancestor a
This tab links to the new contigs index. a Loading contigs dataset can take some time...
Contigs annotation can be downloaded as a table. a
Annotation of each gene can be downloaded in the same format. a
Annotation can be displayed through contigs map. a
Contigs to be displayed can be filtered based on their name, affiliation or size. a
Predicted genes are indicated along the contig in red (unaffiliated), green (affiliated) and orange (similarity to a function domain only). a
Sequences of each OFR are displayed when clicked. a
The corresponding genbank file, contig sequence, and predicted ORFs sequences are available to download. a
All contigs with (at least) an ORF similar to the current contig are listed in this 'Related contigs' table. a
These contigs can then be selected to generate a network. a Network generation can take some time.
The default network displays contigs as gray nodes, and reference genomes as red nodes. a
Color and type of layout are fully customizable. a
This network is made with Cytoscape web, and downloadable as a plot or a network file ready to be imported in Cytoscape.
A click on a network element will display information about the contig, the reference genome, or the BLAST hit. a
Finally, a multiple map viewer is available for nodes selected on network. a
The order of sequences can be modified with a drag-and-drop. Each sequence can also be reverse-complemented. a
A click on a gene will display information about it. a
Multiple viromes can be added to the same network / map gallery. a Again, the first loading step an be a bit long.
Virome nodes can be colored according to the virome. a
Click compare a
The heatmap can also be downloaded as a pdf file. a
This table sums up the affiliation of the different predicted genes. a Genes are highlighted on the map when hovered on the table.
creator: Bonnie L Hurwitz
